lines: The road to the ruin is closed. It has been closed a long time.
Someone should have taken this sign down by now.
Another apprentice? The others went up that road too, once.
Go on, then — one of you has to be the one who comes back.
I keep the gate oiled. Silly, I suppose.
But a gate should open easily for good news.
Mind the webs. The forest keeps what it catches —
travel light, and be worth less to it.
The marsh lights are not stars. Follow them anyway.
They end where the road ends.
Whoever you were before this door, leave it — you will not need it after.
